key_sentences.chinese: 数学比历史难多了，我每天都要复习。
key_sentences.pinyin: Shù xué bǐ lì shǐ nán duō le, wǒ měi tiān dōu yào fù xí.

key_sentences.chinese: 这个科目容易一些，你不用太担心。

key_sentences.english: This subject is a little easier — you don't need to worry too much.

key_sentences.chinese: 她这次的成绩进步了很多，老师很高兴。
key_sentences.pinyin: Tā zhè cì de chéng jì jìn bù le hěn duō, lǎo shī hěn gāo xìng.
key_sentences.english: Her grades improved a lot this time — the teacher is very pleased.

key_sentences.chinese: 他努力复习了一个月，成绩比以前好多了。
key_sentences.pinyin: Tā nǔ lì fù xí le yí gè yuè, chéng jì bǐ yǐ qián hǎo duō le.
key_sentences.english: He studied hard for a month and his grades are much better than before.

key_sentences.chinese: 老师给了我们一些练习题，让我们回家复习。
key_sentences.pinyin: Lǎo shī gěi le wǒ men yì xiē liàn xí tí, ràng wǒ men huí jiā fù xí.
key_sentences.english: The teacher gave us some exercises and asked us to revise at home.

key_sentences.chinese: 你觉得哪个科目最难？我觉得数学比英语难得多。
key_sentences.pinyin: Nǐ jué de nǎ ge kē mù zuì nán? Wǒ jué de shù xué bǐ yīng yǔ nán de duō.
key_sentences.english: Which subject do you find hardest? I think maths is much harder than English.

grammar_points.title: A比B+Adj+多了 — A is much more...than B
grammar_points.explanation: A + 比 + B + Adjective + 多了. The 多了 at the end emphasises a big degree of difference. 数学比历史难多了 = Maths is much harder than history. You can also use 得多 instead: 数学比历史难得多. Both mean 'a lot more / much more'. This is different from simply adding 很 before the adjective, which does not make a comparison.

examples.pinyin: Shù xué bǐ lì shǐ nán duō le.
examples.english: Maths is much harder than history.

examples.pinyin: Jīn tiān bǐ zuó tiān lěng duō le.
examples.english: Today is much colder than yesterday.

exercises.question: Which sentence correctly means 'History is much easier than maths'?
option_pinyin: Lì shǐ bǐ shù xué róng yì duō le.
Lì shǐ bǐ shù xué hěn róng yì.
Lì shǐ hé shù xué yí yàng róng yì.
Lì shǐ méi yǒu shù xué róng yì.

exercises.question: Fill in: 他的成绩比以前好___，进步很大。
What is the difference between 多了 and 得多 in A比B+Adj comparisons?
exercises.correct: Both mean 'much more' — they are interchangeable in this pattern
options: Both mean 'much more' — they are interchangeable in this pattern
多了 means 'a little more'; 得多 means 'much more'
Only 多了 can follow 比; 得多 cannot
得多 is formal; 多了 cannot be used in writing

exercises.question: Translate: 这道题比那道题难多了。
exercises.correct: This question is much harder than that one.
options: This question is much harder than that one.
This question is harder than that one by a little.
This question and that one are equally hard.
That question is harder than this one.

exercises.question: Which word CANNOT be placed at the end of A比B+Adj to mean 'much more'?

mini_exercises.question: What does 多了 after an adjective in a 比 sentence emphasise?
mini_exercises.correct: A big degree of difference (much more...)
options: A big degree of difference (much more...)

mini_exercises.question_english: Maths is much harder than history.

option_pinyin: jīn tiān bǐ zuó tiān rè duō le
jīn tiān bǐ zuó tiān duō le rè
duō le jīn tiān bǐ zuó tiān rè
jīn tiān duō le bǐ zuó tiān rè

grammar_points.title: 些 — Softener / Plural marker
grammar_points.explanation: 些 is used in three main ways depending on what it follows. 一些 Some / a few — placed before a noun: 一些问题 (some problems), 一些人 (some people). These / those — 这些/那些 as plural markers: 这些科目 (these subjects), 那些书 (those books). 对比 After an adjective in comparison: 容易一些 (a little easier) — 些 softens the degree, unlike 多了 which amplifies it.

examples.chinese: 给我一些时间，我需要复习。
examples.pinyin: Gěi wǒ yì xiē shí jiān, wǒ xū yào fù xí.
examples.english: Give me some time — I need to revise.

examples.chinese: 这些科目都很难，但历史容易一些。
examples.pinyin: Zhè xiē kē mù dōu hěn nán, dàn lì shǐ róng yì yì xiē.
examples.english: These subjects are all difficult, but history is a little easier.

exercises.question: What does 容易一些 mean?

Lǎo shī gěi le wǒ men yī xiē hěn.
Lǎo shī yī xiē gěi le wǒ men tí.
Yī xiē lǎo shī gěi liàn xí tí.

exercises.question: What is the difference between 难多了 and 难一些?
exercises.correct: 难多了 means 'much harder'; 难一些 means 'a little harder'
options: 难多了 means 'much harder'; 难一些 means 'a little harder'
难多了 means 'a little harder'; 难一些 means 'much harder'
They mean exactly the same thing
Neither can follow a 比 comparison

zhè wèn tí xiē hěn nán

grammar_points.title: 数量补语 — Quantitative complements
grammar_points.explanation: A quantitative complement tells 'how much' or 'how long' an action happened. Verb + 了 + Quantity: 复习了三个小时 = revised for three hours. 进步了很多 = improved a lot. The complement answers 'by how much' or 'for how long'. 好多了 is a special case where 多了 acts as a degree complement meaning 'much better'. Note: the 了 here indicates completion or change of state, not the future.

examples.chinese: 他复习了两个小时，成绩好多了。
examples.pinyin: Tā fù xí le liǎng ge xiǎo shí, chéng jì hǎo duō le.
examples.english: He revised for two hours and his grades are much better.

examples.chinese: 她的成绩进步了很多，老师很满意。
examples.pinyin: Tā de chéng jì jìn bù le hěn duō, lǎo shī hěn mǎn yì.
examples.english: Her grades improved a lot — the teacher is very satisfied.

Tā yī ge yuè fù xí le nǔ lì.
Tā nǔ lì yī ge yuè le fù xí.

quiz.correct: She revised hard for two hours.
options: She revised hard for two hours.
She has been revising for two days.

quiz.question: What does 比以前好 mean?
Which sentence uses 些 correctly to mean 'a little harder'?
Translate: 哪个科目你觉得最难？
quiz.correct: Which subject do you find the hardest?
options: Which subject do you find the hardest?
Which subject do you like the most?
How many subjects do you study?

quiz.question: What does 成绩进步了 mean?

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 波伏娃，你觉得数学难吗？我觉得数学比历史难多了。
lines.pinyin: Bō Fú Wá, nǐ jué de shù xué nán ma? Wǒ jué de shù xué bǐ lì shǐ nán duō le.
lines.english: Beauvoir, do you find maths difficult? I think maths is much harder than history.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 我也觉得数学难，不过比英语容易一些。哪个科目你最喜欢？
lines.pinyin: Wǒ yě jué de shù xué nán, bú guò bǐ yīng yǔ róng yì yì xiē. Nǎ ge kē mù nǐ zuì xǐ huān?
lines.english: I also find maths hard, but it's a little easier than English. Which subject do you like most?

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 我最喜欢历史，因为历史比数学容易多了，而且很有意思。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ zuì xǐ huān lì shǐ, yīn wèi lì shǐ bǐ shù xué róng yì duō le, ér qiě hěn yǒu yì si.
lines.english: I like history the most because history is much easier than maths and also very interesting.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 那你数学怎么复习？我每次都不知道怎么复习。
lines.pinyin: Nà nǐ shù xué zěn me fù xí? Wǒ měi cì dōu bù zhī dào zěn me fù xí.
lines.english: Then how do you revise maths? I never know how to revise.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 我每天复习一些练习题，做大概两个小时。成绩比以前好多了！
lines.pinyin: Wǒ měi tiān fù xí yì xiē liàn xí tí, zuò dà gài liǎng ge xiǎo shí. Chéng jì bǐ yǐ qián hǎo duō le!
lines.english: I revise some exercises every day — about two hours. My grades are much better than before!

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 太好了！我也要努力复习，希望我的成绩也能进步。
lines.pinyin: Tài hǎo le! Wǒ yě yào nǔ lì fù xí, xī wàng wǒ de chéng jì yě néng jìn bù.
lines.english: Great! I also want to revise hard — I hope my grades can improve too.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 王老师，我这次的数学成绩怎么样？
lines.pinyin: Wáng lǎo shī, wǒ zhè cì de shù xué chéng jì zěn me yàng?
lines.english: Teacher Wang, how were my maths grades this time?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 进步了很多！你的成绩比上次好多了，我很高兴。
lines.pinyin: Jìn bù le hěn duō! Nǐ de chéng jì bǐ shàng cì hǎo duō le, wǒ hěn gāo xìng.
lines.english: You've improved a lot! Your grades are much better than last time — I'm very pleased.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 谢谢老师！我这个月每天都努力复习两个小时。
lines.pinyin: Xiè xie lǎo shī! Wǒ zhè ge yuè měi tiān dōu nǔ lì fù xí liǎng ge xiǎo shí.
lines.english: Thank you, Teacher! I've been revising hard for two hours every day this month.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 很好！不过还有一些地方需要注意。这些题还是有点难。
lines.pinyin: Hěn hǎo! Bú guò hái yǒu yì xiē dì fang xū yào zhù yì. Zhè xiē tí hái shì yǒu diǎn nán.
lines.english: Very good! But there are still some issues to pay attention to. These questions are still a bit hard (for you).

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 我知道了，我会再认真复习这些科目的内容。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ zhī dào le, wǒ huì zài rèn zhēn fù xí zhè xiē kē mù de nèi róng.
lines.english: I understand — I'll revise the content of these subjects more carefully.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 好！只要你继续努力，成绩一定会越来越好的。
lines.pinyin: Hǎo! Zhǐ yào nǐ jì xù nǔ lì, chéng jì yí dìng huì yuè lái yuè hǎo de.
lines.english: Great! As long as you keep working hard, your grades will definitely keep getting better and better.
